rationale.R1.1: The contribution claim is hedged and buried near the end; the gap statement is repeated three times across the motivation paragraphs rather than stated crisply once; 'make this stronger' centrally demands a sharper thesis and a tighter framing of the novelty claim.
rationale.R1.5: Load-bearing. The excerpt presents results and architecture before the introduction/motivation, then revisits the problem statement twice more. The structural disorder is so severe that writing-only polish leaves the piece fundamentally weak; reorganizing into a coherent arc (motivation → approach → results) is required for the delegation to be satisfied.
rationale.R1.6: Load-bearing. Three consecutive paragraphs (paragraphs 3, 5, and 6) each independently restate the same 'ambiguous queries are a problem' observation with minimal variation. Eliminating redundancy and tightening prose at the sentence and paragraph level is the most literal interpretation of 'make this stronger.'
